feature: List CIs with filtering, pagination, and sorting
user_story: As a CMDB operator, I want to list configuration items with type/status/environment/search filters and pagination so that I can browse and locate CIs in large inventories.
expected_behavior: Validates query against queryFiltersSchema (type, status, environment, search + pagination: limit, offset, page, pageSize, sort_by, sort_order) via validateOptional; unknown keys are stripped but every key the handler reads is in the schema so filtering works. Builds a Cypher MATCH (ci:CI) with AND-ed filters and a case-insensitive CONTAINS search on name/id/external_id, computes a total count, and supports page-based or offset-based pagination (limit capped at 1000). Sort field/direction are sanitized via validateCISortField/validateSortDirection to prevent Cypher injection. Returns 200 {_success, _data:[ci...], _pagination{total,_count,_offset,_limit,_page,_pageSize,_totalPages}}; 500 on error.

user_story: As a CMDB user, I want to search CIs by a free-text query so that I can quickly find items by name or attribute.
expected_behavior: Body validated by searchCISchema {query (required, min 1), limit (default 50)}; schema keys match what the handler reads, so it works. Re-validates query is a non-empty string (400) and limit is 1-1000 (400). Runs the Neo4j full-text index CALL db.index.fulltext.queryNodes('ci_search', $query) ordered by score DESC, capped at limit. Returns 200 {_success, _data:[{_ci, _score}], _count, _query}. Depends on the 'ci_search' index existing; 500 otherwise.

user_story: As a CMDB user, I want to retrieve one CI by its ID so that I can inspect its full properties.
expected_behavior: Reads id from the path. Calls neo4jClient.getCI(id); returns 404 {_success:false} if not found, otherwise 200 {_success, _data} where the node properties are passed through convertNeo4jTypes (Neo4j Integer/DateTime coercion and stripping of leading underscores from property keys). The id-missing 400 branch is effectively unreachable because the route requires :id. 500 on error.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to create a configuration item so that new infrastructure is tracked.
expected_behavior: Body validated by ciInputSchema (id, name, type required; status defaults to 'active'; external_id/environment/discovered_at optional; metadata defaults to {}). Handler double-checks id/name/type and returns 400 if missing. Persists via neo4jClient.createCI(req.body) and returns 201 {_success, _data, _message}. If the underlying error message includes 'already exists' it returns 409 Conflict; other errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to update a CI's mutable fields so that the record stays accurate.
expected_behavior: Body validated by updateCISchema (name, type, status, environment, metadata all optional). Reads id from path, first fetches the CI via getCI and returns 404 if it does not exist, then calls neo4jClient.updateCI(id, req.body) and returns 200 {_success, _data, _message}. 500 on error.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to delete a CI so that decommissioned items are removed from the graph.
expected_behavior: Reads id from path, fetches the CI first and returns 404 if not found, then runs Cypher MATCH (ci:CI {id:$id}) DETACH DELETE ci (which also removes all attached relationships) and responds 204 No Content with an empty body. 500 on error.

user_story: As a CMDB user, I want to see a CI's incoming/outgoing relationships so that I understand how it connects to other CIs.
expected_behavior: Reads direction (default 'both') and depth (default 1) from query. Validates direction is in/out/both (400) and depth 1-10 (400), confirms the CI exists (404), then calls neo4jClient.getRelationships(id, direction, depth) and maps each result into {id, type, source_ci_id, target_ci_id, source_ci, target_ci, properties, created_at} based on the Neo4j start node. Returns 200 {_success, _data, _count, _depth}. However the route only validates relationshipQuerySchema {direction} so the depth query param is stripped and never reaches the handler — multi-hop traversal is impossible via the API.
issue_note: ci.routes.ts:31-33 relationshipQuerySchema omits 'depth'; validate() uses stripUnknown:true (validators.ts:36) so ?depth=N is dropped before getCIRelationships (ci.controller.ts:320) reads it, leaving depth permanently defaulted to 1.

user_story: As a CMDB user, I want to see what a CI depends on (upstream) so that I can plan changes and understand prerequisites.
expected_behavior: Reads depth (default 5) from query validated by depthQuerySchema (matches handler). Validates depth 1-10 (400), confirms CI exists (404), then calls neo4jClient.getDependencies(id, depth) and returns 200 {_success, _data, _count, _depth}. 500 on error. Schema and handler key names agree, so the depth param works here.

user_story: As a change manager, I want an impact analysis for a CI so that I can gauge the blast radius before making changes.
expected_behavior: Reads depth (default 5, validated 1-10 -> 400) and confirms CI exists (404). Computes downstream impacted CIs via neo4jClient.impactAnalysis(id, depth) and upstream CIs via getDependencies(id, depth), de-duplicating upstream nodes by walking path segments. Returns 200 {_success, _data:{ci, upstream, downstream, impact_score, affected_environments}, _totalImpacted, _depth}, where impact_score = min(totalImpacted/10, 1.0) and affected_environments is the distinct set of involved environments. 500 on error.

user_story: As an auditor, I want the change history of a CI so that I can review who changed what and when.
expected_behavior: Reads limit (default 100, validated 1-1000 -> 400). Obtains the Postgres-backed audit service via getAuditService(postgresClient['pool']) and calls auditService.getCIAuditHistory(id, limit). Returns 200 {_success, _data, _count}. No route-level validation middleware, so the limit param is read directly from req.query. 500 on error.

user_story: As a CMDB user, I want to list relationships filtered by type/from/to/CI so that I can audit how CIs are connected.
expected_behavior: Intended: filter by type, from_id, to_id, ci_id with limit/offset pagination, returning {_success, _data:[{_from_id,_from_name,...,_type,_properties}], _pagination}. In practice the route's listRelationshipsQuerySchema defines underscore-prefixed keys (_type, _from_id, _to_id, _ci_id, _limit, _offset) while the controller reads the non-prefixed names (type, from_id, to_id, ci_id, limit, offset). Because validate() strips unknown keys and replaces req.query, every client filter param is dropped and the handler sees undefined; the endpoint always returns the unfiltered first 100 relationships and ignores limit/offset.
issue_note: relationship.routes.ts:14-21 uses _-prefixed keys; relationship.controller.ts:17-24 reads non-prefixed keys; with stripUnknown:true (validators.ts:36) all filter and pagination params are silently discarded, so filtering and pagination are non-functional.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to create a typed relationship between two CIs so that dependencies are recorded.
expected_behavior: Body validated by relationshipSchema {from_id, to_id, type (one of the 8 RelationshipTypes) required, properties optional}; handler keys match. Re-checks required fields (400), validates type against the allowed list (400), confirms both source and target CIs exist (404 each), rejects self-relationships (400), then persists via neo4jClient.createRelationship and returns 201 {_success, _data, _message}; 409 if the error indicates it already exists. Defect: the success response sets _from_type/_to_type from fromCI._type/toCI._type, but getCI returns properties keyed 'type' (not '_type'), so those fields are always undefined/omitted.
issue_note: relationship.controller.ts:192,195 read fromCI._type/toCI._type, but getCI returns raw node properties with key 'type', so _from_type/_to_type in the 201 payload are always undefined.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to delete a relationship so that obsolete connections are removed.
expected_behavior: Route has an optional :id. If a path id is supplied the handler always returns 400 ('Deletion by relationship ID not supported. Use from_id, to_id, and type query parameters.') — so the documented DELETE /relationships/:id form is a dead path. Deletion only works via DELETE /relationships?from_id=X&to_id=Y&type=Z: it MATCHes the relationship, returns 404 if absent, deletes it, and returns 200 {_success, _message, _data}; missing params -> 400. There is no validation middleware on this route, so type is interpolated directly into the Cypher pattern [r:${type}] without being checked against the allowed RelationshipType list.
issue_note: relationship.controller.ts:238-245 makes the :id form unconditionally 400 (dead route form); lines 250 & 267 interpolate the unvalidated query 'type' into Cypher ([r:${type}]) with no allow-list check (unlike createRelationship), enabling Cypher injection.

user_story: As a CMDB user, I want to list all relationships of a given type so that I can review a specific connection class (e.g. DEPENDS_ON).
expected_behavior: Reads :type from path plus limit (default 100) and offset (default 0) from query (read directly; this route has no validation middleware so pagination works). Validates :type against the 8 allowed RelationshipTypes (400 if invalid), counts and returns paginated results via Cypher MATCH (from:CI)-[r:${type}]->(to:CI) ordered by from.name,to.name. The interpolated type is safe because it is validated first. Returns 200 {_success, _data:[{_from_id,...,_type,_properties}], _pagination, type}. 500 on error.

user_story: As a CMDB user, I want to search CIs by query plus type/status/environment/metadata filters so that I can run targeted lookups.
expected_behavior: Intended: accept query (required) plus optional type/status/environment/metadata_filters and limit/offset, build a dynamic Cypher WHERE, and return matching CIs with pagination. In reality the endpoint cannot succeed: advancedSearchSchema requires _query (underscore-prefixed) while the controller reads req.body.query. Sending {query:...} -> validate strips it and fails _query 'required' (400 Validation Error). Sending {_query:...} -> passes validation but the handler sees query undefined and returns 400 'Search query is required'. Additionally the Cypher param object binds _query/_limit/_offset while the query uses $query/$limit/$offset, so even past validation the bindings would be wrong.
issue_note: search.routes.ts:14-22 requires _-prefixed keys; search.controller.ts:17-25 reads non-prefixed keys; with stripUnknown:true no request body satisfies both validation and the handler, making the endpoint unreachable for success. Param object (lines 42-46) also uses _query/_limit/_offset vs $query/$limit/$offset in the Cypher.

user_story: As a CMDB user, I want a full-text search over CIs so that I can find items ranked by relevance.
expected_behavior: Intended: run db.index.fulltext.queryNodes('ci_search', $query) ordered by score DESC, limited, returning {_success, _data:[{_ci, _score}], _count, _query}; if the index is missing it returns a 500 with a configuration message. In reality the endpoint is unreachable for success: fulltextSearchSchema requires _query while the controller reads req.body.query. Sending {query} -> stripped, _query 'required' fails (400); sending {_query} -> passes but handler sees query undefined -> 400 'Search query is required'.
issue_note: search.routes.ts:24-27 requires _query; search.controller.ts:146 reads req.body.query; stripUnknown:true means no body satisfies both layers, so the endpoint always returns 400.

user_story: As a CMDB user, I want to find CIs of one type related to CIs of another type via a relationship so that I can answer pattern questions (e.g. apps hosted on servers).
expected_behavior: Intended: given ci_type, relationship_type, related_ci_type (+ limit), MATCH (ci:CI {type:$ci_type})-[:relationship_type]->(related:CI {type:$related_ci_type}) and return distinct ci. In reality unreachable for success: relationshipSearchSchema requires _ci_type/_relationship_type/_related_ci_type while the controller reads ci_type/relationship_type/related_ci_type. Sending non-prefixed -> stripped, required _-fields fail (400); sending _-prefixed -> passes but handler sees them undefined -> 400 'Missing required fields'. Also the param object binds _limit vs the $limit used in Cypher.
issue_note: search.routes.ts:29-34 requires _-prefixed keys; search.controller.ts:228 reads non-prefixed keys; stripUnknown:true makes the endpoint always 400. relationship_type is also interpolated into Cypher (line 245) but is unreachable due to the 400.

user_story: As a CMDB data steward, I want to list CIs that have no relationships so that I can find disconnected or incomplete records.
expected_behavior: Runs Cypher MATCH (ci:CI) WHERE NOT (ci)-[]-() to count and page orphaned CIs ordered by created_at DESC, returning 200 {_success, _data:[{_id,_name,_type,...,_metadata}], _pagination}. With no query params it works using defaults (limit 100, offset 0). However orphanedQuerySchema defines _limit/_offset while the handler reads limit/offset, so when a client supplies ?limit/?offset those keys are stripped by validate() and the handler falls back to defaults — pagination params are silently ignored.
issue_note: search.routes.ts:36-39 uses _limit/_offset; search.controller.ts:302 reads limit/offset; with stripUnknown:true client-supplied pagination is discarded and defaults (100/0) always apply.
